more: n. m. S. देश, Pr. disā ; Pālī, deso . 1. Country; territory; region. Des des kā pānī pīyā hai . He has drunk the water of many countries. (He has great experience). 2. An air sung at midnight. des-āchār, des byohār, des-chalan , n. m. 1. Local usage or observance. 2. Public policy; custom or fashion of the country. des-adhkārī , n. m. The chief governor or superintendant of a province or country. des-ānt, deshānt , n. f. The boundaries of a country or district. des-bhāshā, desh-bhākhā , n. f. The particular dialect or language of a country; the vernacular. des ba-des phirnā , v. n. To travel about; see the world. des-tiyāg ; A. hijrat , n. m. 1. Abandoning one's country; expatriation. 2. Emigration. des chhoṛ'nā, hijrat k. , v. n. To leave one's country or home; to emigrate. Bidhnā ne ek purakh banāyā, tiryā dī, aur neh lagāyā. Chūk hāi kachhū vā se aisī, des chhoṛ huā par-desī . Riddle.…

def: mythological
wety: دیوْ مالا (dev mālā, “mythology”) + ـی (-ī)

ety: Pehl. dev; Zend daêva, fr. dīv; S. देव
def: s.m. An evil spirit, devil, demon, an evil jinn, a ghost, hobgoblin; a giant, a monster, a huge fellow or thing
more: n. m. 1. A giant; monster; a big strong man. 1. Ādmī kyā hai? deo hai! 2. Yeh kām unke deo se bhī na hogā . 2. A devil ( jin , 1.). deo-zād , Demon-born. Yeh ghoṛā deo-zād hai! deo kā deo . A great giant. Deo kā deo ā khaṛā huā .

n. m. S. देव, , Pr. devvam, daivam . Lat. Deus , Gr. Theos . A god. 1. Māno to deo, nahīṅ bhīt kā leo . Prov. Believe and he is a god, if not he is only plaster. 2. Harshe deo bilok chhabi, barkhahīṅ suman apār, Jai, jai! Pirbhu gun gayān bal dhām haran mahīṅ bhār . Rāmayan. 3. Ghar ke khīr khāëṅ, deotā bhalā māneṅ! Prov. The milk mess eaten up all, the god propitiated all the same! (She persuades herself that she has secured the favor of the god when she has made in name only the offering which she has herself eaten). deo-uṭhān, diṭhvan, deo uṭhnī ekādash , n. The 11th day of the light fortnight of Kātak on which Vishnu wakes from his sleep of four months. dev-asthān', deval , n. m. A Hin. temple.
s. m. 1. A god, a deity. ديوتهان dew-thān ( देवस्थान ) s. f. A temple, place of idols. 2. ( met. ) The rain.
subs: vulg. deʼo-bād, s.f. lit. 'A devil's wind,' a whirlwind, 'a devil'
vulg. deʼo-stān, s.m. The habitation of demons
s.m. A perfect giant, a veritable monster.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دیوْ (dēw).

def: s.m. A deity, a god ( = dev).
more: s. m. 1. A god, a deity. ديوتهان dew-thān ( देवस्थान ) s. f. A temple, place of idols. 2. ( met. ) The rain.

def: s.m. The Butea frondosa (syn. palās. The tree in full bloom presents a striking spectacle, like fire on the horizon; from natural fissures and incisions made in the bark, issues, during the hot season, a red juice, which soon hardens into a ruby-coloured, brittle, astringent gum, similar to kino, and sold as Bengal kino; lac is collected on the branchlets; the leaves are used as plates, &c., and instead of paper, to wrap up parcels, and are given as fodder to buffaloes; the flowers, with alum, are made into the yellow dye used in the Holī festival, and are also used medicinally; the seeds are given as purgative and anthelmintic, mostly in veterinary practice; the wood is used for coating wells, and for the sacrificial fire)
more: s. m. Name of a tree. (Butea frondosa).

def: s.m. Shove, push, knock, jostle, jolt, jog, shock, impact, collision; a blow or stroke of misfortune, or affliction, &c.; loss (in trade), damage
more: n. m. 1. A shove; push; jolt 2. Impetus in coitu . 3. Damage; loss. 4. Calamity ( āfat ). dhakkā denā , v. a. 1. To shove; push. 2. To turn out; shut the door on one. 3. To bring any calamity on one. Kyā tere dinoṅ ne dhakkā dīyā hai? dhakke khānā , v. n. 1. To be pushed, knocked, kicked about. Ikke chaṛh-ke jahāṅ jāë, paise de-ke dhakke khāë . Prov. Whene'er in ekka you go out, For money paid you're knocked about. 2. To wander about. Kahāṅ dhakke khātā phirtā hai? dhakkā lagnā . v. n. 1. To suffer a shock. 2. See ṭoṭā uṭhānā , 1. Baiṭhe biṭhāë sau rupaë kā dhakkā lag gayā . 3. To meet with misfortune, etc; to turn out ill; to go to the wall. dhakā pelī k. , v. a. To push and shove; to jostle. dhakkam dhakkā, dhakā pel . Shoving and jostling. Melā meṅ baṛ dhakkam dhakkā bhel Mag. dinoṅ ko dhakke denā , v. a. To live with difficulty; to get along somehow.
s. m. A shove, jolt, push, jog. دهکادينا dhakkā denā, To shove, push, jolt.

def: v.t. To cause to be covered, to have or get covered, &c.

n. m. 1. A slope; declivity. 2. A gradient. 3. Condition; state. 4 Mode ( ḍaul ). 5. A mould. 6. Rus . Habit ( bān , 3.). 1. Ḍhāl paṛā tainu baiṭh khān kā, yoī bahānā kartā saë, Parjā-pat sab bhar bhar lāveṅ, koū ḍūb na martā saë . W. Rus. song. Your habit's just to sit and eat, and make excuses lame, The carriers bring full loads each day, no harm to them e'er came. 2. Paras bhīṅt ḍhāne kā tan ne ke paṛā ḍhālā, re baldā? Mat bhīṅṭ paras ḍhā, re baldā! Rus. What are you at, Oh ox! do you want to break the wall of meeting hall? Dont rub against the wall! 6. (S. ढाल ) A shield; buckler. ḍhāl talvār , n. f. lit . shield and sword. Arms. ḍhāl ḍhāl jānā , v. n. To go or walk slowly. ḍhāl sā , ( Bot .) Peltate; peltated.
more: adj. Like; as. Ghī sākar tanne lā dūṅ jākar, yo saë chāl kisāṅṛāṅ kī, Khā le, Kāman! tauṅ ghī sākar, po-kar ḍhāl kisāṅṛāṅ kī . Rus. song. I'll bring you ghī and sugar, such our rural custom is, Feast, fair one! on our ghī and sweets cooked as our custom is!

def: v. n. H. ḍhīl loose. 1. To be slackened or loosened. 2. To run down; flow. Āṅsū ḍhalaknā . 3. To be spilt or poured out. 4. To go or hang down. 1. Chhātiyāṅ ḍhalak gaīṅ . 2. Jin pīpal kā pātā toṛā, chaṛhā huā voh to ḍhalkā. Mat toṛo pātā pīpal kā ! Hin. Rus. song. Who breaks a pīpal leaf though prosperous he shall wane, From plucking Pīpal leaves your hands you should restrain.
